title: My Therapist Watched Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: My Therapist Watched Evangelion             Quote Post by cl_theo » Thu May 16, 2013 5:02 pm I've been seeing my therapist for about 1 1/2 years. The guy really helped me through some bad times. About half a year ago, I opened up and told him how Evangelion was my favorite show of all time and changed my life. I explained to him all the themes and characters, and it sparked his interest. For the past few months, we've been watched the original show at the sessions, and he just recently finished watched EoE. He loved it. I plan to get more information for him next session and get specifics on what he liked and hated about the show for discussion. One thing I should note, is his reaction to THAT scene. Hell I had to ask him directly about his reaction. He thought it seemed out of place considering the show already had enough drama and themes going on at the time. At the same time, it also did sort of set the tone for the rest of the movie. Next session, I'll get a more in-depth opinion from him and how he viewed the characters from therapists standpoint. In the meantime, have you guys ever shown Evangelion to people with psych degrees or in a similar field? Also are there any questions you want me to ask him?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 16, 2013 5:05 pm You got a therapist to watch Evangelion? That's impressive. I can't say that I've ever shown it to somebody who is a professional in that field, but I look forward to seeing some of his in-depth reactions.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu May 16, 2013 5:12 pm This is probably a history-making moment of this site that we actually get a PROFESSIONAL opinion of the nitty-gritty details of Evangelion. I'm looking forward to this with sheer anticipation.

[]:             Quote Post by cl_theo » Thu May 16, 2013 5:17 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: You got a therapist to watch Evangelion? That's impressive. I can't say that I've ever shown it to somebody who is a professional in that field, but I look forward to seeing some of his in-depth reactions.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu May 16, 2013 5:21 pm The topic of this thread really sounded like the set-up to a gag - "and now he's seeing a therapist" Anyway, it would be awesome to get a professional diagnosis of the characters and which characters he finds most interesting. I would be interested if he can second-guess things such as why Asuka is how she is before watching 22. And being a man of science (sort of) I bet he prefers Ritsuko to Misato

[]:             Quote Post by cl_theo » Thu May 16, 2013 5:27 pm Jayfive wrote: The topic of this thread really sounded like the set-up to a gag - "and now he's seeing a therapist" Anyway, it would be awesome to get a professional diagnosis of the characters and which characters he finds most interesting. I would be interested if he can second-guess things such as why Asuka is how she is before watching 22. And being a man of science (sort of) I bet he prefers Ritsuko to Misato

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu May 16, 2013 5:27 pm xanderkh wrote: This is probably a history-making moment of this site that we actually get a PROFESSIONAL opinion of the nitty-gritty details of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by lastcody » Thu May 16, 2013 5:28 pm This is really interesting I can't wait to hear his thoughts

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu May 16, 2013 5:39 pm cl_theo wrote: The whole looking nothing like her mother kept making him forget who she was later on I never asked him his favorite characters for some reason. I'll totally ask him. Actually let me edit my first post in case there's any questions you guys would like me to ask him!

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu May 16, 2013 6:13 pm It would've been interesting to ask the psychiatrist I used to go to for many years about Evangelion, but I wasn't introduced to the series until a couple years after we decided I didn't need meds or therapy any longer.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu May 16, 2013 7:12 pm Wait... he watched them with you durring sessions? So you basically paid him to watch the show.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu May 16, 2013 7:45 pm NemZ wrote: Wait... he watched them with you durring sessions? So you basically paid him to watch the show.

[]:             Quote Post by cl_theo » Thu May 16, 2013 7:47 pm esselfortium wrote: It's no wonder he loved watching it, then

[]:             Quote Post by Nyanlathotep » Thu May 16, 2013 10:15 pm I would pay someone to watch Eva. I mean in a professional setting, of course. I tried to get my therapist to watch it and I don't know if she ever did. D:

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Fri May 17, 2013 12:15 am That sounds interesting What did he thought of the action arc and the Jet Alone episode? First time watching that is. I know some people were unimpressed with that arc despite it having foreshadowing. Though even then the Magma and Jet alone arc seem quite disliked with many. Also, this stuff is on the side right? Hes fine with watching it during session time?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Fri May 17, 2013 5:13 am esselfortium wrote: It's no wonder he loved watching it, then

[]:             Quote Post by cl_theo » Fri May 17, 2013 2:42 pm SimplyMason0 wrote: That sounds interesting What did he thought of the action arc and the Jet Alone episode? First time watching that is. I know some people were unimpressed with that arc despite it having foreshadowing. Though even then the Magma and Jet alone arc seem quite disliked with many. Also, this stuff is on the side right? Hes fine with watching it during session time?

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri May 17, 2013 4:18 pm Wow...Nice work. Another innocent drafted to our cause. On a side note, I got my history teacher to start watching it. He's only seen the first episode, but so far he said he really likes it. He's also (wisely) watching it subbed.

[]:             Quote Post by cl_theo » Sat May 18, 2013 6:20 pm CJD wrote: Poor Brik. Don't worry man, I didn't forget about you. Well, excluding the above, I'm pretty sure I've seen some other members mentioning bringing it up with their therapist. I also recall someone mentioning that, many moons ago, an anime magazine showed the show to a doctor in the psych field (don't recall whether he was a iatrist or ologist), and had him offer his opinions. The only thing I recall from that discussion, though, was that the doctor apparently said that Rei, if she didn't get help, was at danger of being a sociopath or something? On my end, I've never shown it to my therapist but I have talked about it with him. I actually think that was one of the earliest things we talked about.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat May 18, 2013 6:26 pm cl_theo wrote: Even though I trust the guy, it's embarrassing to say a Japanese animation show from the 90's changed the way I look at life.
